SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.model.state

Class GameOverState

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.model.state.GameOverState
All Implemented Interfaces:
GameState
public class GameOverState extends Object implements GameState
Game over state ignoring gameplay input and preserving final score (State Pattern).

This state is entered when the game ends (board fills up or level fails). In this state, all gameplay input events are ignored, but the final board state and score are preserved for display. The state handles new game requests by transitioning back to PlayingState.

State Behavior:

  • All movement/rotation events return current view data without processing
  • Pause requests are ignored (returns self)
  • New game requests transition to PlayingState
Author:
Dong, Jia.

    Constructor
    Description

    Constructs a GameOverState without state context.
    GameOverState(Board board, GuiController guiController, GameStateContext stateContext)
    Constructs a GameOverState with state context.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameOverState

      public GameOverState(Board board, GuiController guiController)
      Constructs a GameOverState without state context.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board instance, must not be null
      guiController - the GUI controller for UI updates, must not be null

    • GameOverState

      public GameOverState(Board board, GuiController guiController, GameStateContext stateContext)
      Constructs a GameOverState with state context.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board instance, must not be null
      guiController - the GUI controller for UI updates, must not be null
      stateContext - the state context for state transitions, may be null

  • Method Details

    • onDownEvent

      public DownData onDownEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Handles the DOWN event during game over. Ignores the event and returns current view data without processing.
      Specified by:
      onDownEvent in interface GameState
      Parameters:
      event - the move event (ignored)
      Returns:
      DownData with null clear row and current view data

    • onLeftEvent

      public ViewData onLeftEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Handles the LEFT event during game over. Ignores the event and returns current view data.
      Specified by:
      onLeftEvent in interface GameState
      Parameters:
      event - the move event (ignored)
      Returns:
      ViewData containing the current board state

    • onRightEvent

      public ViewData onRightEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Handles the RIGHT event during game over. Ignores the event and returns current view data.
      Specified by:
      onRightEvent in interface GameState
      Parameters:
      event - the move event (ignored)
      Returns:
      ViewData containing the current board state

    • onRotateEvent

      public ViewData onRotateEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Handles the ROTATE event during game over. Ignores the event and returns current view data.
      Specified by:
      onRotateEvent in interface GameState
      Parameters:
      event - the move event (ignored)
      Returns:
      ViewData containing the current board state

    • onRotateCCWEvent

      public ViewData onRotateCCWEvent(MoveEvent event)
      Handles the ROTATE_CCW (counterclockwise) event during game over. Ignores the event and returns current view data.
      Specified by:
      onRotateCCWEvent in interface GameState
      Parameters:
      event - the move event (ignored)
      Returns:
      ViewData containing the current board state

    • handlePauseRequest

      public GameState handlePauseRequest()
      Handles a request to pause the game. Pausing during game over is not applicable, so returns self.
      Specified by:
      handlePauseRequest in interface GameState
      Returns:
      this GameOverState instance (no state change)

    • handleNewGameRequest

      public GameState handleNewGameRequest()
      Handles a request to start a new game. Transitions to PlayingState and initializes a new game.
      Specified by:
      handleNewGameRequest in interface GameState
      Returns:
      the PlayingState instance for the new game
